ai_summary: The Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) reported robust Q1FY'27 performance with 15.9% year-on-year electricity traded volume growth, primarily driven by record power demand from persistent heatwaves and significant price increases across major market segments. A material red flag is the drastic 81.4% year-on-year decline in Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) volumes, indicating severe market illiquidity due to a sharp drop in sell bids, which could hinder India's green energy transition and IEX's related offerings. This suggests a strong underlying energy demand but raises concerns about the health and liquidity of the renewable energy certificate market.
